refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve or exceed the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a single performance metric: operational availability. It is expressed in a solution-neutral way, specifying what the system must do without implying how. The refinement clarifies the time frame (agreed evaluation period) to ensure measurability and completeness.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve or exceed the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability
Incorporate real-time system health monitoring and predictive maintenance
Design modular subsystems to isolate faults and enable quick repair
Establish operational procedures and automated controls to minimize downtime
Use robust and proven design elements to reduce failure rates
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy reduces dependency on single components, minimizing coupling between failure modes
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy as a DP supports independence by allowing one component to take over if another fails, thus maintaining availability without tightly coupling subsystems.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that redundancy may increase system complexity leading to new failure modes
Assumes operational policies support maintenance of redundant components
Risk that monitoring and maintenance procedures may not be fully effective in practice
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and maintenance DPs to complement redundancy and further improve availability assurance.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system safety, including failover and redundancy mechanisms

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Used to notify other modules or systems of safety-related events or failover activations

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status data and sends safety status updates for monitoring

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module, contains many unrelated functions, leading to high diffusion of failover logic.
Contains physical components but failover logic is more safety-related than vehicle-specific.
Focused on remote control, failover is not central here, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Highly cohesive for failover and redundancy; minimal diffusion outside this module.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The failover capability is best contained within the Safety Services module because it directly relates to system safety and redundancy, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may create coupling.
If failover requires coordination with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, interface complexity may increase.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If failover mechanisms require significant interaction with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, consider defining clearer interface contracts or splitting failover logic into submodules within Safety Services to maintain cohesion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability' directly addresses the FR of achieving or exceeding the customer-defined operational availability target by providing failover mechanisms to maintain system operation during component failures. This mapping is logically sound and grounded in the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may create coupling.
If failover requires coordination with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, interface complexity may increase.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2) for failover and redundancy logic, indicating strong cohesion and minimal scattering of the DP implementation. Other modules have higher diffusion scores, suggesting that centralizing failover logic in Safety Services reduces diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible since Safety Services exposes interfaces such as SafetyAlert and Telemetry that enable monitoring and notification of failover events, supporting traceability and validation of operational availability targets.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the failover DP to the Safety Services IM is clear and appropriate, as the module scope explicitly includes failover and redundancy mechanisms, aligning well with the FR's safety and availability goals.

recommended_revisions: If failover mechanisms require significant interaction with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, define clearer interface contracts to manage dependencies and reduce coupling.
Consider splitting failover logic into submodules within Safety Services to maintain high cohesion if complexity grows due to interactions with other modules.
Review and monitor telemetry data dependencies to ensure they do not introduce unintended diffusion or coupling risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and alignment. The DP of implementing redundant system components directly addresses the FR with a clear and logically sound mapping. The IM allocation to Safety Services is appropriate with high containment and low diffusion, supporting verification feasibility and allocation clarity. Although some diffusion risks and interface coupling concerns exist, they are manageable through recommended revisions and do not necessitate further decomposition at this time. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and cohesive module boundary is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: To address potential coupling and diffusion risks, clarify interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules and consider submodule decomposition within Safety Services if complexity increases.

assumptions: Failover mechanisms are sufficiently encapsulated within Safety Services module.
Telemetry data dependencies can be managed through interface contracts.
open_risks: Potential coupling due to telemetry data dependencies.
Interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Verification results of failover functionality through Safety Services interfaces.
Monitoring data on diffusion and coupling metrics post-implementation.
